rationale: The cited 2008 JMRK paper is directly aligned with the citing paper’s stated gap: prior sampling work focused on effects of sampling a single experiential cue, whereas the 2010 paper tests sequential order across multiple samples. The constructs strongly overlap (product sampling, incentive/desirability effects, downstream choice/consumption), suggesting the citation is likely a substantive theoretical antecedent rather than a perfunctory mention. The DOI-level match (10.1509/jmkr.45.4.403) confirms this exact target reference.
